key_highlights: Global tech selloff deepens, with AI-related stocks plunging amid rising doubts about valuations and a stronger dollar
Iran-US peace talks progress, leading to a sharp drop in oil prices below $75 and recovery of Hormuz Strait traffic
ECB warns inflation may stay above 2% for an extended period, diverging from Fed's rate stance and pressuring the euro
SpaceX stock tumbles over 20% from highs, erasing billions in market value as retail investors face losses
Gold prices stabilize but bulls cut outlooks amid tech rout and geopolitical shifts
Qualcomm nears $4B deal for AI chip startup Modular Inc, signaling continued M&A in AI sector
Former Fed Chairman Alan Greenspan dies at 100, marking end of an era in monetary policy

sectors.trend: Sharp selloff driven by AI valuation concerns, rising dollar, and profit-taking after prolonged rally. Major indices like Nasdaq and S&P 500 futures dive, with Korean tech shares plunging 10%. Intel CEO gives reality check, and SpaceX tumbles 23% from highs.
sectors.implications: Investors are rotating out of high-growth tech into defensive assets. AI hype is being tested by macro headwinds (strong dollar, Fed rate uncertainty). M&A activity like Qualcomm-Modular shows long-term confidence but near-term volatility is extreme.

sectors.trend: Oil prices fall below $75 for first time since March as Iran-US peace talks progress and Hormuz traffic resumes. US waivers allow Iran to woo Asian buyers, increasing supply expectations. Aluminum and other metals also tumble amid tech-led bear mood.
sectors.implications: Lower oil prices ease inflationary pressures but hurt energy sector stocks. Geopolitical risk premium is fading, benefiting import-dependent economies. However, Chubb CEO flags ongoing disruption threats, so caution remains.

sectors.trend: Mixed: ECB's Lane warns inflation above 2% for some time, diverging from Fed and pressuring euro. Euro hits August low. JPMorgan and Chevron highlighted as cheap stocks by Jim Cramer. Monte Paschi CEO welcomes interest in bank. AllianzGI raises $744M for APAC private credit.
sectors.implications: Rate divergence creates FX volatility and opportunities in currency markets. European banks face margin pressure from ECB hawkishness, while US banks benefit from higher rates. Private credit continues to attract capital.

sectors.trend: Becton Dickinson issues voluntary recall for skin prep products. CVS under scrutiny after healthcare report. Kraft Heinz and Kimberly-Clark present at consumer conference. Costco adds new frozen Greek yogurt cups.
sectors.implications: Defensive sectors like healthcare and staples are relatively stable amid tech rout. Recalls and regulatory scrutiny create stock-specific risks. Consumer staples companies focus on innovation and cost management.

sectors.trend: Bitcoin and Ethereum prices moving higher. Ex-Trump advisor makes bold case for Bitcoin. Solana predicted to be top DeFi crypto by 2030. Crypto policy roundup in Washington.
sectors.implications: Crypto showing resilience amid traditional market selloff, possibly as a hedge. Regulatory clarity in US could boost institutional adoption. Solana's DeFi narrative gains traction.

sectors.trend: Best CD rates up to 4% APY, money market accounts up to 4.01% APY. Mortgage rates mixed. Fed rate reality check hits stocks. Yen intervention risk rises after Katayama-Bessent talks.
sectors.implications: Rising rate environment continues to benefit savers but pressures growth stocks. Bond yields remain elevated, with central bank divergence creating arbitrage opportunities. Yen intervention risk adds FX volatility.

sectors.trend: Nissan shelves development of electric Qashqai in cost-cutting drive. Porsche sticks to profit outlook with leaner lineup. Tesla on autopilot involved in fatal crash.
sectors.implications: EV transition faces headwinds from cost pressures and safety concerns. Traditional automakers are recalibrating EV strategies. Porsche's discipline is positive for margins.

sectors.trend: Aluminum leads tumble in metals as tech selloff fuels bear mood. Gold stabilizes but bulls cut outlooks; Deutsche Bank follows Goldman's cut. Silver rebounds slightly.
sectors.implications: Industrial metals under pressure from growth fears and strong dollar. Gold losing safe-haven appeal as geopolitical risks ease and rates stay high. Silver remains volatile.

key_themes: AI valuation correction and tech rout
Geopolitical de-escalation: Iran talks and oil price decline
Central bank divergence: ECB hawkish vs Fed cautious
Strong dollar pressuring risk assets and emerging markets
Defensive rotation into fixed income and consumer staples
Crypto resilience amid traditional market weakness
M&A activity in AI and energy sectors
Regulatory and safety concerns in healthcare and auto
Rising retail investor losses in high-profile stocks like SpaceX
Monetary policy legacy: death of former Fed Chair Greenspan

risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce exposure to high-multiple tech stocks; increase allocation to value and defensive sectors; consider hedging with put options on QQQ or XLK.
Maintain diversified energy exposure; consider long positions in oil futures or ETFs (USO) as a hedge; monitor Hormuz traffic and Iran talks closely.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Central Bank Policy Divergence / Strong Dollar
risk_assessment.mitigation: Hedge FX exposure in EUR and JPY; favor US assets; consider long USD positions via UUP; reduce EM debt exposure.
Allocate to TIPS, commodities, and real assets; avoid long-duration bonds; favor companies with pricing power.
Avoid speculative retail favorites; diversify across sectors; use stop-losses on volatile positions.
Reduce exposure to Korean equities and ETFs (EWY); monitor for further volatility; consider hedging with VIX.
Avoid stocks with pending recalls; monitor BDX and CVS for further developments; favor diversified healthcare ETFs.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy the dip in select AI infrastructure plays with strong fundamentals
opportunities.rationale: Qualcomm's $4B Modular deal signals long-term AI demand. Pullback in tech creates entry points for leaders with cash flows and strategic M&A.

opportunities.recommendation: Increase allocation to energy stocks with strong dividends and low valuation
opportunities.rationale: Oil price decline is overdone; geopolitical risks remain. Chevron and Devon Energy offer yield and activist interest. Energy Transfer's new agreements add growth.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in high-yield savings and CDs to lock in 4%+ yields
opportunities.rationale: Rates remain elevated; locking in now provides risk-free returns amid market volatility. Best for cash reserves.

opportunities.recommendation: Accumulate Bitcoin and Ethereum on dips as institutional adoption grows
opportunities.rationale: Crypto showing resilience; ex-Trump advisor bullish case and Washington policy roundup suggest regulatory clarity. Solana DeFi narrative adds upside.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider long positions on euro via EUR/USD if ECB hawkishness persists
opportunities.rationale: Euro at August low; ECB's Lane and Escrivá signal persistent inflation, which may force rate hikes. Divergence with Fed could narrow.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce tech exposure and rotate into consumer staples and healthcare
defensive_moves.rationale: Tech rout likely to continue; defensive sectors like Kraft Heinz, Kimberly-Clark, and Costco offer stability and pricing power.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge portfolio with gold or gold miners despite recent cuts
defensive_moves.rationale: Gold stabilized; if tech selloff deepens, safe-haven demand may return. Deutsche Bank and Goldman cuts may be contrarian indicators.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase cash position and use money market funds
defensive_moves.rationale: Market volatility high; cash yields 4%+ provide optionality to deploy capital during further dips.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid speculative retail favorites like SpaceX
defensive_moves.rationale: SpaceX down 23% from highs, retail investors losing money. Michael Burry sees $3 trillion problem. Wait for stabilization.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Short or avoid Korean equities and EM tech
defensive_moves.rationale: Korean shares plunge 10% with extreme volatility; Chinese stocks in Hong Kong enter bear market. Contagion risk high.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Bearish with high volatility. Tech selloff likely to continue as AI valuations adjust to higher rates and strong dollar. Oil prices may stabilize around $70-75 if Iran deal holds. Defensive rotation into staples, healthcare, and fixed income will persist. Crypto may outperform traditional risk assets. Central bank divergence will keep FX markets active.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: Cautiously optimistic. AI infrastructure spending (Qualcomm-Modular) and energy transition (NatPower-Tesla, CATL sodium batteries) provide long-term growth. Inflation may moderate as oil falls, allowing Fed to pause. Geopolitical risks remain but de-escalation in Iran is positive. Greenspan's death may prompt reflection on monetary policy but no immediate impact.
key_catalysts: US-Iran peace talks outcome and Hormuz traffic normalization
Fed and ECB interest rate decisions and inflation data
Q2 earnings season, especially for tech and AI companies
US dollar strength and its impact on multinational earnings
Regulatory developments in crypto and AI
SpaceX and other high-profile stock recoveries or further declines
Korean market stabilization or further contagion
Oil price trajectory and impact on global inflation
Consumer spending data amid summer sales events
US election primaries and policy implications
watch_list: WTI and Brent crude oil prices
Nasdaq 100 and S&P 500 tech sector performance
ECB and Fed speeches (Lane, Lagarde, Powell)
SpaceX stock (if publicly traded via SPAC or direct listing)
